_README: Dataset B (sectors half). The SA-specific context a decision-maker carries: who regulates them, what incentives/levers move them, what a deal here must respect. AUTHORED from each sector's public regulator/charter/SETA frame. qlfs_industry is the REAL QLFS 'Indus' label whose mgr/professional rows supply the persona's demographic BODY (verified 2026-06-15). govt shares Community industry with NGO, so its split is ASSIGNED, not read from QLFS — note assign_split. decision_lens='commercial' uses ROI/cost logic; govt uses 'procedural' (tender/compliance) logic instead. REVIEW the sectors closest to your real customers — I grounded these from public sources but you know your buyers.

banking.label: Banking & Financial services
banking.qlfs_industry: Financial intermediation; insurance; real estate and business services
deal_must_respect: reputational caution typical of regulated finance
banking.notes: Slow, risk-averse, compliance-heavy buying. Fintech pitches land here. Inclusion + FS-charter angles can unlock budget that pure ROI can't.
banking.governance_frame: King IV (apply-and-explain) + Companies Act s76 directors' fiduciary duty to act in the best interests of the company. Decision-makers must weigh stakeholder interests, risk governance and ethics — not just return.
scoreable_levers: FS-sector B-BBEE Charter scorecard points (ownership, ESD, access to financial services)
financial-inclusion targets that count toward regulatory/charter standing
King IV stakeholder & ethics outcomes the board reports against
banking.in_org_interest_means: A proposal serves the bank's interest when it advances commercial return AND defends regulatory standing/conduct AND earns charter/inclusion credit. A pitch that scores on only one of these stalls — e.g. great ROI but POPIA/conduct risk dies at risk committee.
banking.risk_committee_gate: Material vendor/data decisions pass a risk & compliance committee before commercial sign-off — this is why banking buying is slow and why the compliance_csr function carries unusual veto weight here.
banking.decision_provenance: King IV + Companies Act s76 + FS-sector B-BBEE Charter (authored)

retail.label: Wholesale & Retail
retail.regulator: —  (Consumer Protection Act applies; no single sector regulator)
retail.notes: Afoody's grocer/category-buyer target. Moves on margin and velocity; ESD points can sweeten a supplier deal.

mining.label: Mining & Resources
mining.notes: THIN body pool (47) — personas recombine from a small QLFS slice; flag if showcasing. SED/CSR officer is the partnership seat (compliance_csr function). SLP/charter credit is the real currency, often above commercial ROI.

foodbev.label: Food & Beverage / Manufacturing
foodbev.notes: Producers/suppliers behind the retail shelf. Cost, reliability and food-safety compliance dominate.

ict_telecoms.label: ICT / Telecoms
ict_telecoms.notes: Fast-moving vs banking, but integration- and security-conscious. Champion (end-user) function matters more here — technical practitioners gate adoption.

ngo.label: NGO / Civil society
ngo.assign_split: NGO vs Govt cannot be told apart by QLFS industry — assign this tag deliberately at sample time; mark occupation_provenance accordingly.
ngo.notes: Afoody's food-security-NGO partner (the 'David' profile). Moves on impact-per-rand and donor-reportable outcomes, not profit.

govt.label: Government / Public sector
govt.assign_split: Shares Community industry with NGO — assign deliberately, mark provenance.
govt.regulator: National Treasury (PFMA/MFMA); B-BBEE & PPPFA procurement rules
govt.notes: DISTINCT logic: the question is not 'good ROI' but 'will this survive a tender and an audit'. decision_lens='procedural' — criteria are compliance/process/B-BBEE, not commercial return. Do not let a govt persona reason like a private buyer.
